title: Koyamacalia shikokiana
intro: Koyamacalia shikokiana, a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, is native to Japan and is also known as Japanese mountain mint. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Koyamacalia shikokiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used as an ingredient in various herbal formulations, such as infusions and decoctions.
taxonomy: Koyamacalia shikokiana is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Japan and was previously classified as a member of the genus Senecio before being reclassified as Koyamacalia in 2010. Koyamacalia shikokiana is a perennial plant that grows in shaded areas, particularly in the mountains of Shikoku. The plant produces small white flowers and has been used in traditional Japanese medicine. Koyamacalia shikokiana is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and overcollection.
